case_title: HOUSING AUTHORITY OF SAN FRANCISCO vs. CRISP, DENNIS E. AKA DENNIS CRISP et al

docket_entries.description: COURT TRIAL CONTINUED TO JAN-11-1996 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 206 PER DEPARTMENT AT TRIAL

DEPT. 2, RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, JUDGE; B. YEE, CSR. 11:20 AM C/C FOR COURT TRIAL. PLAINTIFF & PLAINTIFF'S ATTY, PRESENT; JOSEPH MALANDRA, DEFENDANT'S ATTY, PRESENT, DEFENDANT NOT PRESENT. 11:22 AM DEFENDANT'S ORAL MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING: DEFENDANT TO EXECUTE SUBSTITUTION OF ATTY NO LATER THAN 2/2/96 & FILED. IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO EXECUTE & FILE SUBSTITUTION OF ATTY, DEFENDANT'S ATTY TO FILE APPLICATION & ORDER SHORTENING TIME HEAR MOTION TO WITHDRAW AS ATTY OF RECORD & TRIAL TO BE SET FOR 2/5/96 @ 10:30 AM, DEPT. 2. PLAINTIFF TO WAIVE NOTICE. FINALLY, UPON WITHDRAWAL, DEFENDANT'S ATTY TO NOTIFY DEFENDANT THAT HE WILL BE REPRESENTING HIMSELF AT TRIAL. 12:00 NOON COURT ADJOURNED.
DEFT'S ATTY ON VACATION TO 1-9-96, PER PLTF'S ATTY. PLTF ORAL MOT TO CONTINUE GRANTED. CASE CONT'D TO SET ONLY. PLTF TO GIVE NOTICE.

DEPT. 2, RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, JUDGE; V.T. INVENTADO, CLERK; BELDON YEE, CSR. C/C FOR COURT TRIAL. ALL PARTIES PRESENT, WITNESSES WERE SWORN & EXAMINED & EXHIBITS WERE MARKED, IDENTIFIED & ADMITTED. IN WEIGHING THE EVIDENCE, THE COURT FINDS PLAINTIFF HAS SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN. JUDGMENT FOR PLAINTIFF AGAINST DEFENDANT FOR POSSESSION & RESTITUTION OF THE PREMISES AT 1880 PINE ST, #603, SF, CA, DAMAGES & BACK RENTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1393.90 & COSTS PER MEMORANDUM OF COSTS.
CLERKS NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND AFFIDAVIT OF MAILING FILED

PROOF OF SERVICE ON SUMMONS & COMPLAINT, PREJUDGEMENT CLAIM TO RIGHT OF POSSESSION; FILED BY PLTF; ON 30-OCT-95; BY POSTING AND MAILING; ON ALL OCCUPANTS.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT CRISP, DENNIS E. AKA DENNIS CRISP THE RESTITUTION AND POSSESSION OF THE PREMISES: 1880 PINE STREET, APT.603, SAN FRANCISCO, CA $1,393.90 RENT, TOTAL JUDGMENT $1,393.90, LEASE FORFEITED, COST PER COST BILL.
